idea.hook: How long would it take to reach the nearest star?
facts: Excluding our Sun, the closest star is Proxima Centauri, 4.24 light-years away.
That's approximately 40 trillion kilometers (25 trillion miles).
The fastest human-made object, the Parker Solar Probe, reached a top speed of 635,266 km/h (394,736 mph) relative to the Sun.
idea.payoff: Even at that record-breaking speed, it would still take over 7,000 years to reach Proxima Centauri! The universe is unimaginably vast.
idea.title: How Long To Reach The NEAREST STAR? 🤯🚀

scenes.text: How long to reach the nearest star?
scenes.visual: A stylized question mark pulses over a dark, star-filled space background. Subtle streaks of light move across the screen.
Two glowing star icons (one labeled 'Sun', the other 'Proxima Centauri') appear with a dotted line connecting them. The distance '4.24 Light-Years' appears along the line.
Large numbers '40,000,000,000,000 km' animate and expand slightly, with a smaller '25 trillion miles' underneath. The background shows a vast, distant galaxy.

scenes.text: Fastest object: Parker Solar Probe at 635,266 km/h.
scenes.visual: An animated icon of the Parker Solar Probe zips across the screen from left to right, leaving a faint trail. A digital speedometer dial animates to show the top speed.

scenes.text: Still over 7,000 years! The universe is unimaginably vast.
scenes.visual: A large, bold '7,000 YEARS!' appears prominently, pulsing. Below it, 'The universe is unimaginably vast.' A deep space background with countless tiny stars and distant nebulae emphasizes the scale.
